title: Crispy Black Bean Patties
subtitle: with Avocado Spinach Salad & Lime Aioli
ingredients: 3 tbsp Follow Your Heart® Soy-Free Vegenaise®

steps.text: Drain and rinse the black beans. Peel and mince the garlic. Peel and finely dice just ½ cup onion. Dice the cucumber. Halve the grape tomatoes. Zest, halve, and juice the lime.
Add just ¼ tsp minced garlic, just ½ tsp lime zest, just 2 tsp lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl and mix the lime aioli.

Add black beans, remaining minced garlic, diced onion, garbanzo bean flour, zhoug seasoning, and ½ tsp salt to a large bowl. Mix with your hands, crushing the beans, until dough is sticky and uniform. Form dough into 6 patties, each about 2 inches wide.
Heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil to a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add black bean patties and cook until crisp and heated through, 3 to 5 minutes per side.

Add baby spinach, remaining lime juice, 2 tsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a large bowl, and toss. Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and thinly slice the flesh. Divide spinach salad between bowls and top with black bean patties, diced cucumber, halved tomatoes, and sliced avocado. Drizzle with lime aioli. Bon appétit!

tips: * These ingredients will be split between steps.
TIP: Save the remaining onion for your own use.
TIP: You can use a food processor for this step if you'd prefer.
TIP: Add more oil as necessary to crisp the patties.

Aioli vs Mayo—What's the Difference? Both have a creamy consistency and whitish color. But mayonnaise is an emulsion made with egg yolk and oil, and aioli is an emulsion created with just garlic and extra virgin olive oil. Sometimes egg yolk is added as well, but even then, the addition of garlic sets aioli apart from mayo. In this recipe we put our own spin on things by making lime aioli, which includes minced garlic, lime zest, lime juice, Vegenaise, and a pinch of salt. It's so good!
